repo_prior_art.cite: none; eid_lingam_direction_min_order_v1 is a non-Gaussian cumulant-direction result, not Gaussian-ANM score-Jacobian local geometry

published_prior_art.cite: rolland2022score supplies the population leaf functional and zhu2023sample supplies a finite-sample SCORE guarantee, but neither derives the mirror-path C_m(delta)-to-Chernoff expansion

repo_prior_art.cite: none; the catalogue contains no likelihood order-error exponent indexed by a SCORE diagonal-Jacobian gap

published_prior_art.cite: chernoff1952measure gives the simple-product testing exponent; the added content is calibration by the new mirror-path score-gap calculation

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue local causal-order decision limit for a Gaussian-ANM mirror path

published_prior_art.cite: hoyer2008nonlinear and peters2014anm give qualitative ANM asymmetry, while standard local-testing machinery yields the elbow once the proposed geometry is established
hoyer2008nonlinear identifies the linear-Gaussian reversible exceptional boundary

zhu2023sample analyzes a particular SCORE finite-sample guarantee and koehler2023efficiency concerns density-parameter efficiency, not this cross-fitted diagonal-Jacobian contrast or its training-random-L4 limitation
koehler2023efficiency studies score-matching versus likelihood for density parameters, not conditional large deviations of the stated causal-order statistic

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorems 1-3 / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Chernoff frontier and moving-alternative elbow concern only the explicitly constructed fixed two-node mirror Gaussian-ANM path, not a uniform class of nonlinear ANMs.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: A pointwise local causal-order testing characterization under the stated mirror-path regularity conditions.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Proposition 2 and Open-ended Question 1 / ass:jacobian-estimability
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Feasible score-template conclusions are conditional on existence of cross-fitted diagonal score-Jacobian estimators with the declared training-random L4 rate; the proposed L4 premise is not asserted to imply exponent attainment.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Sign consistency holds conditionally under r_n=o(sqrt(C_m(delta_n))); the exact-exponent question is conditional on additional exponential-stability conditions for the specified template.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Extend the mirror calculation to a normalized nondegenerate smooth tangent class and derive uniform constants relating C_m(delta) to Chernoff information, or prove that C_m alone cannot support such a uniform frontier.

Resolve the open template rung with a concrete conditional exponential-stability condition that is sufficient for Chernoff-exponent attainment, then test necessity using an explicit score-sieve estimator.
Add a finite-n corollary translating C_m(delta) into a sample-size requirement and quantitatively compare it with the curvature-margin guarantee of zhu2023sample.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 under the accepted pointwise and conditional-estimator scope; retain the field framing and prioritize a concrete exponent-attainment condition for the score template.
